meaning: n.野蛮,未开化,原始,不文明
examples: It towered above the older buildings like a citadel, which indeed it was, the outpost of a new age, perhaps a new civilization, perhaps a new barbarism.
We do not ask for the death penalty: barbarism must not be met with barbarism.
Mankell asks, “Why does barbarism always wear a human face?”
Staring an advanced civilization straight in the face, the Roman dismissed it as either barbarism or decadence.
He was influenced by a contemporary, Maulana Maudoodi in India, who was also repelled by modernity and saw it as barbarism.
